text: Single-pixel imaging offers a transformative approach to capturing scenes without a direct line of sight, yet achieving high image fidelity remains difficult, particularly in environments with significant ambient noise. Here, we demonstrate a robust single-pixel imaging method that reconstructs high-quality images by systematically acquiring their Fourier spectrum. Our approach utilizes four-step phase-shifting sinusoidal structured illumination to sample specific spatial frequencies. By applying an inverse Fourier transform to the resulting spectrum, we can faithfully reconstruct the target scene. Because this technique relies on the integrated light collection rather than spatial resolution at the detector, it allows for flexible sensor placement, enabling imaging even when the detector is positioned around corners or behind diffusers. Furthermore, the strategy exploits the inherent sparsity of natural images in the Fourier domain, allowing for accurate image reconstruction from sub-Nyquist measurements. Experimental results confirm that this method effectively suppresses environmental noise and eliminates the direct-current component, producing clear, high-resolution images even under challenging illumination conditions where traditional single-pixel methods typically struggle.
